perturbation.original_text: eDiets shall be solely responsible for ensuring the timeliness, accuracy, and correctness of all facts, data and information ("Facts") contained within the eDiet Content. eDiets shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Women.com, its officers, directors, employees and agents from and against all damages, awards, costs, fees (including reasonable attorneys' fees), and expenses that such parties may incur as a result of third party claims arising from such third parties' reliance on Facts. Women.com retains the right, but not the obligation, to independently corroborate, or have corroborated, the timeliness, accuracy, and correctness of all Facts.
perturbation.changed_text: eDiets shall not be responsible for ensuring the timeliness, accuracy, and correctness of all facts, data and information ("Facts") contained within the eDiet Content. Women.com retains the sole right and obligation to independently corroborate, or have corroborated, the timeliness, accuracy, and correctness of all Facts.
perturbation.explanation: By altering Section 2.5 to remove eDiets' responsibility for the 'Facts' and making Women.com solely responsible, it creates a contradiction with Section 12.2 (a) where eDiets warrants that the eDiets Content may be used by Women.com without violating or infringing the rights of any person or entity, including correctness of data. If Women.com is solely responsible for corroborating facts, it weakens eDiets warranty, making it unclear who is responsible for damages from inaccurate data, specifically infringing rights of any person or entity, including correctness of data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: eDiets hereby represents and warrants to and for the benefit of Women.com and its Affiliates that: (a) the eDiets Content may be used by Women.com as permitted by this Agreement without violating or infringing the rights of any person or entity, including, without limitation, rights of privacy or publicity, contractual rights, copyright, trademark, or other Intellectual Property rights; (b) publication of the eDiets Content by Women.com as permitted by this Agreement will not constitute defamation, product disparagement or trade libel; (c) the use of the eDiets Marks by Women.com as permitted by this Agreement will not infringe or violate the trademark, service mark or trade dress rights of any Person; and (d) during the Term of this Agreement, the pages of the eDiets Site to which the Gateway Page provides links shall operate in accordance with the specifications set forth on Exhibit D. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Affiliate" shall mean another entity that controls, is controlled by or is under common control with such party; provided, however, that such entity is not a human being. For purposes of this definition, the terms "controls," "is controlled by," or "is under common control with," refer to the power to direct the policies and day-to-day operations of an entity, whether by virtue of ownership of a voting securities, contract, or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly removes eDiets' responsibility for the accuracy of the 'Facts' and assigns it solely to Women.com. This directly contradicts section 12.2(a), where eDiets warrants that the eDiets Content can be used by Women.com without infringing on any rights, including the correctness of data. If Women.com is now solely responsible for corroborating the facts, it weakens or nullifies eDiets' warranty, creating a conflict regarding who is liable for damages arising from inaccurate data. This creates an ambiguity and directly undermines the enforceability of eDiets' warranty in Section 12.2(a).

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of Women.com's developing, maintaining and promoting the Diet Center pursuant to this Agreement, beginning on the tenth (10th) day after the execution of this Agreement and thereafter on the last day of each month during the Term of this Agreement, eDiets shall pay to Women.com a monthly fee in the amount set forth on Exhibit C unless adjusted as set forth in Section 3.1 [Advertising and Promotion]. Following the Initial Term, Women.com shall have the right, upon no fewer than sixty (60) days prior written notice to eDiets, to increase the amount of the Payment Schedule; provided, that (i) Women.com may not increase the Payment Schedule more than once in any period of twelve (12) consecutive months; and (ii) such increase may not exceed twenty percent (20%) of the then current Payment Schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of Women.com's developing, maintaining, but not promoting the Diet Center pursuant to this Agreement, beginning on the tenth (10th) day after the execution of this Agreement and thereafter on the last day of each month during the Term of this Agreement, eDiets shall pay to Women.com a monthly fee in the amount set forth on Exhibit C unless adjusted as set forth in Section 3.1 [Advertising and Promotion]. Following the Initial Term, Women.com shall have the right, upon no fewer than sixty (60) days prior written notice to eDiets, to increase the amount of the Payment Schedule; provided, that (i) Women.com may increase the Payment Schedule as many times as possible in any period of twelve (12) consecutive months; and (ii) such increase may exceed twenty percent (20%) of the then current Payment Schedule.
perturbation.explanation: By removing Women.com's obligation to 'promote' the Diet Center in Section 6.1, and by saying it can increase the payment schedule as many times as possible and exceed 20%, this creates a direct conflict with Exhibit C. Exhibit C says it is $37.00 net advertising CPM and an overall net CPM of $28.57 with all elements to the point it's a 2 year deal. This intext contradiction in Section 6.1 makes the Payment Schedule and cost metrics of Exhibit C highly uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PAYMENT SCHEDULE Pricing/Delivery: PRICING AS PART OF THIS PACKAGE ONLY Flight: 2 Year Deal Net Advertising CPM: $37.00 (Just Co-Branded Banners) Overall Net CPM $28.57 (With all other elements) Total Net Cost: $2,000,000 (Per Year)/$4,000,000 (Two Years) Total Guaranteed Impressions: 70,000,000 (Per Year)/140,000,000 (TwoYears) YEAR 1 10 days after Effective Date-Production $250,000 Last day of each of the 1st through 12/th/ months after the Launch Date $145,833 Media Component $138,750 Other (Promotions, production, etc) $7,083 YEAR 2 Last day of each of 13/th/ through 24/th/ months after the Launch Date $166,666 Media Component $138,750 Other (Promotions, production) $27,916.66 Total Payment to Women.com $4,000,000
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for Women.com to 'promote' the Diet Center and allows them to increase the payment schedule as many times as possible and exceed 20%. This directly contradicts Exhibit C, which specifies a fixed pricing structure (Net Advertising CPM: $37.00, Overall Net CPM $28.57) and a total cost of $4,000,000 for a two-year deal. By altering the obligations and payment terms, the changed text creates a direct conflict with the fixed pricing and deliverables outlined in Exhibit C. The fact that Women.com no longer needs to 'promote' the diet center, combined with the ability to raise rates above 20% whenever they want creates uncertainty regarding the cost metrics of exhibit C which is an intext contradiction and fits the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Women.com, in its sole discretion, may withdraw specific or general permission to use the Women.com Marks upon seventy-two (72) hours written notice to eDiets. Upon expiration of such notice period, eDiets shall immediately discontinue use of the applicable Women.com Marks. As used herein, "Women.com Marks" means all trademarks, service marks and corporate and brand identification and indicia, including without limitation word marks, logos and other picture marks, phrases, jingles, composite marks, corporate, commercial and institutional images, product designations and identifications of Women.com, whether registered or not.
perturbation.changed_text: Women.com, in its sole discretion, may withdraw specific or general permission to use the Women.com Marks upon seventy-two (72) hours verbal notice to eDiets. Upon expiration of such notice period, eDiets shall use the applicable Women.com Marks. As used herein, "Women.com Marks" means all trademarks, service marks and corporate and brand identification and indicia, including without limitation word marks, logos and other picture marks, phrases, jingles, composite marks, corporate, commercial and institutional images, product designations and identifications of Women.com, whether registered or not.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying Section 9.1 to allow Women.com to give 'verbal notice' for withdrawing permission to use its marks, and compelling eDiets to 'use the applicable Women.com Marks' after the notice period, creates a conflict with the obligation set forth in 10.3(e). Section 10.3(e) dictates both parties shall eliminate from their respective web sites and/or respective web pages any marks or branding related to the other party on termination, which directly contradicts Section 9.1 in the modified text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the termination, expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement in accordance with its terms (the "Termination Date"): (a) the rights and licenses granted by each party to the other pursuant to this Agreement shall automatically terminate; (b) where applicable, each party shall return to the other party, within thirty (30) days from such Termination Date, all intellectual property, technology or other property in its possession used in connection with this Agreement that is proprietary to the other party; (c) Confidential Information shall be returned in accordance with the terms of Section 11; (d) within sixty (60) days after such Termination Date, Women.com shall remove and cease to use all eDiets Content provided or made available for use or display on the Gateway Page pursuant to or in connection with this Agreement; (e) within sixty (60) days after such Termination Date, each party shall eliminate from their respective web sites and/or respective web pages any marks or branding related to the other party (i.e., the eDiets Marks and the Women.com Marks, respectively) used in connection with this Agreement; 8 (f) within sixty (60) days after such Termination Date, Women.com shall purge from its servers and systems all eDiets Content; (g) within sixty (60) days after such Termination Date, Women.com shall make-good to eDiets any and all payments made to Women.com under this Agreement for advertising inventory not yet delivered or run, provided that eDiets has paid for such inventory; and (h) within sixty (60) days after such Termination Date, eDiets shall pay to Woman.com any and all payment due under this Agreement for delivered advertising and promotions inventory in accordance with Sections 3.1 [Advertising and Promotion] and 6.1.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 9.1 to allow Women.com to give 'verbal notice' (instead of written) for withdrawing permission to use its marks, and compels eDiets to 'use the applicable Women.com Marks' (instead of discontinuing use) after the notice period. This directly contradicts Section 10.3(e), which states that upon termination of the agreement, each party shall eliminate from their respective web sites and/or respective web pages any marks or branding related to the other party. The change in Section 9.1 forcing eDiets to continue using Women.com marks creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict with the obligation to remove those marks upon termination per Section 10.3(e).
